AI is used in many areas of our business and we plan to further incorporate AI into additional areas. The use of AI offers efficiencies, but also introduces significant challenges related to data security, privacy, intellectual property
developments in our use of process automation and artificial intelligence (AI) further heighten our dependency on technology, as such technology may be complex and unpredictable

Failure to successfully integrate AI technologies, respond to client or market demands, identify or address applicable legal or regulatory issues or effectively manage related risks could result in legal and regulatory liabilities and harm our reputation and growth.
AI technologies, including generative AI, may create content that appears correct but is factually inaccurate or flawed.
Globally, courts and regulatory agencies are developing approaches to dealing with AI-related issues, which creates uncertainty around the use of the technology.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI spend, headcount, or named programs disclosed in excerpts; scored 3 based on named but unquantified deployment and expansion plans.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI use cases are named only at a high level (process automation, efficiencies) with no deployment metrics or specific product/initiative names; excerpts are predominantly risk-section disclosures, not operational detail.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Scored 4 because specific risk mechanisms are described (regulatory liability, reputational harm, inaccuracy/bias, integration failure) with causal pathways to business impact, but no quantification or named governance bodies appear in these excerpts.
